scan_Cross-Site Request Forgery_0.title: Missing CSRF Token on Form
scan_Cross-Site Request Forgery_0.description: A POST form at http://testaspnet.vulnweb.com/Comments.aspx?id=0 does not include a CSRF token, making it vulnerable to cross-site request forgery attacks.

scan_Sensitive Data Exposure_1.title: Missing HTTPS Encryption
scan_Sensitive Data Exposure_1.description: The application is served over unencrypted HTTP instead of HTTPS.

scan_XXE Injection_2.title: XML External Entity Injection (XXE)
scan_XXE Injection_2.description: The endpoint processes XML input with external entity resolution enabled, allowing reading of server files and SSRF.

scan_Vulnerable Components_3.title: Vulnerable IIS Version: 8.5
scan_Vulnerable Components_3.description: IIS version 8.5 is outdated and has known vulnerabilities.

scan_Broken Access Control_6.title: Potential IDOR: id
scan_Broken Access Control_6.description: Parameter "id" may be vulnerable to Insecure Direct Object Reference — different IDs return different content without authorization checks.

scan_Broken Access Control_7.title: HTTP Method Override Accepted
scan_Broken Access Control_7.description: The server processes X-HTTP-Method-Override headers, potentially bypassing method-based access controls.

scan_Insecure Deserialization_8.title: Serialized Data in Hidden Field: __VIEWSTATE (.NET ViewState)
scan_Insecure Deserialization_8.description: The hidden field "__VIEWSTATE" contains .NET ViewState serialized data.

scan_Insecure Deserialization_9.title: ASP.NET ViewState Without MAC Validation
scan_Insecure Deserialization_9.description: The ASP.NET ViewState field lacks MAC validation, allowing tampering and potential remote code execution via deserialization attacks.

scan_Logging & Monitoring_10.title: Missing Security Reporting Headers
scan_Logging & Monitoring_10.description: Missing security reporting mechanisms: Report-To, NEL (Network Error Logging).

scan_Security Misconfiguration_11.title: Missing Security Headers (6)
scan_Security Misconfiguration_11.description: The following security headers are missing: Strict-Transport-Security, Content-Security-Policy, X-Content-Type-Options, X-Frame-Options, Referrer-Policy, Permissions-Policy.

scan_Security Misconfiguration_12.title: Server Technology Disclosure
scan_Security Misconfiguration_12.description: The server discloses technology details: Server: Microsoft-IIS/8.5; X-Powered-By: ASP.NET; X-AspNet-Version: 2.0.50727. Version numbers are exposed, aiding targeted attacks.

scan_Security Misconfiguration_13.title: Dangerous HTTP Methods Enabled
scan_Security Misconfiguration_13.description: The server allows dangerous HTTP methods: TRACE.

scan_Race Condition_14.title: Potential Race Condition: http://testaspnet.vulnweb.com/Comments.aspx?id=0
scan_Race Condition_14.description: 9/10 concurrent duplicate POST requests were all accepted, suggesting the endpoint lacks proper concurrency controls.

scan_Clickjacking_15.title: Missing Clickjacking Protection
scan_Clickjacking_15.description: The page has no X-Frame-Options header and no CSP frame-ancestors directive. It can be embedded in an iframe on any website.

scan_scan-configuration_16.title: Scan Profile: No Profile Selected
scan_scan-configuration_16.description: No scan profile is configured. Recommended profile: "Standard Scan" — Full testing suite at medium depth — recommended for most targets.

scan_WSTG-SESS-02: Cookie Attributes_17.title: Session Cookie Missing Security Attributes: ASP.NET_SessionId
scan_WSTG-SESS-02: Cookie Attributes_17.description: The session cookie "ASP.NET_SessionId" is set without important security attributes: missing SameSite attribute.

scan_Security Configuration_18.title: Missing Content-Security-Policy Header
scan_Security Configuration_18.description: A Content-Security-Policy (CSP) header was not found in the HTTP response. CSP is the primary defence-in-depth mechanism against Cross-Site Scripting (XSS) and data injection attacks by whitelisting trusted content sources.

scan_Security Configuration_19.title: Missing HTTP Strict Transport Security (HSTS)
scan_Security Configuration_19.description: The Strict-Transport-Security (HSTS) header is absent.  Without HSTS, browsers may connect over plain HTTP, exposing users to SSL-stripping attacks and man-in-the-middle interception of credentials and session tokens.

scan_Security Configuration_20.title: Missing X-Content-Type-Options Header
scan_Security Configuration_20.description: The X-Content-Type-Options header is absent.  Without this header, browsers may MIME-sniff responses and interpret them as a different content type, enabling content-injection and drive-by-download attacks.

scan_Security Configuration_21.title: Missing X-Frame-Options Header
scan_Security Configuration_21.description: The X-Frame-Options (XFO) header is absent.  Without it, attackers may embed the page in a hidden iframe and conduct clickjacking attacks that trick users into performing unintended actions.

scan_Security Configuration_22.title: Missing Referrer-Policy Header
scan_Security Configuration_22.description: The Referrer-Policy header is not present.  Without it, browsers may leak sensitive URL information (tokens, session IDs) in the Referer header to third-party sites and analytics endpoints.

scan_Security Configuration_23.title: Missing Permissions-Policy Header
scan_Security Configuration_23.description: The Permissions-Policy header (formerly Feature-Policy) is absent. Without it, the page grants browser feature access (camera, microphone, geolocation, payment, etc.) by default, broadening the attack surface.

scan_Insecure Deserialization_24.title: Serialized Data in Hidden Field: __VIEWSTATE (.NET ViewState)
scan_Insecure Deserialization_24.description: The hidden field "__VIEWSTATE" contains .NET ViewState serialized data.

scan_Insecure Deserialization_25.description: The ASP.NET ViewState field lacks MAC validation, allowing tampering and potential remote code execution via deserialization attacks.

scan_Insecure Deserialization_118.description: The ASP.NET ViewState field lacks MAC validation, allowing tampering and potential remote code execution via deserialization attacks.

scan_Logging & Monitoring_119.description: Missing security reporting mechanisms: Report-To, NEL (Network Error Logging).

scan_Clickjacking_120.description: The page has no X-Frame-Options header and no CSP frame-ancestors directive. It can be embedded in an iframe on any website.

scan_Security Configuration_121.title: Missing Content-Security-Policy Header
scan_Security Configuration_121.description: A Content-Security-Policy (CSP) header was not found in the HTTP response. CSP is the primary defence-in-depth mechanism against Cross-Site Scripting (XSS) and data injection attacks by whitelisting trusted content sources.

scan_scan-configuration_127.title: Scan Profile: No Profile Selected
scan_scan-configuration_127.description: No scan profile is configured. Recommended profile: "Standard Scan" — Full testing suite at medium depth — recommended for most targets.

scan_Security Configuration_128.title: Missing Content-Security-Policy Header
scan_Security Configuration_128.description: A Content-Security-Policy (CSP) header was not found in the HTTP response. CSP is the primary defence-in-depth mechanism against Cross-Site Scripting (XSS) and data injection attacks by whitelisting trusted content sources.

scan_Security Configuration_129.title: Missing HTTP Strict Transport Security (HSTS)
scan_Security Configuration_129.description: The Strict-Transport-Security (HSTS) header is absent.  Without HSTS, browsers may connect over plain HTTP, exposing users to SSL-stripping attacks and man-in-the-middle interception of credentials and session tokens.

scan_Security Configuration_130.title: Missing X-Content-Type-Options Header
scan_Security Configuration_130.description: The X-Content-Type-Options header is absent.  Without this header, browsers may MIME-sniff responses and interpret them as a different content type, enabling content-injection and drive-by-download attacks.

scan_Security Configuration_131.title: Missing X-Frame-Options Header
scan_Security Configuration_131.description: The X-Frame-Options (XFO) header is absent.  Without it, attackers may embed the page in a hidden iframe and conduct clickjacking attacks that trick users into performing unintended actions.

scan_Security Configuration_132.title: Missing Referrer-Policy Header
scan_Security Configuration_132.description: The Referrer-Policy header is not present.  Without it, browsers may leak sensitive URL information (tokens, session IDs) in the Referer header to third-party sites and analytics endpoints.

scan_Security Configuration_133.title: Missing Permissions-Policy Header
scan_Security Configuration_133.description: The Permissions-Policy header (formerly Feature-Policy) is absent. Without it, the page grants browser feature access (camera, microphone, geolocation, payment, etc.) by default, broadening the attack surface.

scan_XXE Injection_134.title: XML External Entity Injection (XXE)
scan_XXE Injection_134.description: The endpoint processes XML input with external entity resolution enabled, allowing reading of server files and SSRF.
